heading: Receiving loan from court officer
label: 647 Receiving loan from court officer
text: Whoever knowingly receives, from a clerk or other officer of a court of the United States, as a deposit, loan, or otherwise, any money belonging in the registry of such court, is guilty of embezzlement, and shall be fined under this title or not more than the amount embezzled, whichever is greater, or imprisoned not more than ten years, or both; but if the amount embezzled does not exceed $1,000, he shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 187 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 10035 Stat. 1107The punishment provision of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., now , was substituted for the words “punished as prescribed in ” and the smaller punishment for an offense involving $100 or less was inserted. (See reviser’s notes under sections 641 and 645 of this title.)section 185 of title 18section 646 of this titlesection 185 of this title

Amendments1996— substituted “$1,000” for “$100”.Pub. L. 104–2941994—, substituted “shall be fined under this title or not more than the amount embezzled, whichever is greater, or imprisoned” for “shall be fined not more than the amount embezzled or imprisoned”.Pub. L. 103–322, § 330016(2)(G), substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $1,000” after “he shall be”.Pub. L. 103–322, § 330016(1)(H)
